back_translation: “Amen, amen, I say to you, the one who does not enter through the gate into the sheep pen, but climbs in by another way, that one is both a thief and a robber.
But the one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
The gatekeeper opens for that one, the sheep listen to that one's voice, and that one calls his own sheep one by one by name, then leads them out.
When he has brought all his own sheep out, that one walks ahead first, and his sheep follow, because they know his voice.
The flock will not follow a stranger at all, but will flee from him, because the sheep do not know the voice of a stranger.”
Jesus told them this parable, but they did not understand what what He spoke to them meant.
So Jesus said to them again, “Amen, amen, I say to you, I am the gate of the sheep.
All those who came before Me were thieves and robbers, but the flock did not listen to them.
I am the gate. If anyone enters through Me, he will be saved. He will be able to come in and go out, and will find pasture.
A thief comes only to steal, kill, and destroy. But I have come that the flock may have life, and have it abundantly.
I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
A hired man who is not the shepherd, and who is not the owner of the sheep — when he sees a wolf coming, he abandons the flock and flees, and the wolf snatches and scatters them.
Because he is a hired man, and is not concerned for the flock.
I am the good shepherd, and I know My sheep, and My sheep know Me,
just as the Father knows Me, and I know the Father. And I lay down My life for the sheep.
I still have other sheep that are not from this fold. I must bring those also, and they will listen to My voice. They will become one flock, with only one shepherd.
For this reason the Father loves Me — because I lay down My own life, in order to take it up again.

No one takes it from Me, but I Myself am the one who lays it down voluntarily. I have authority to lay down My life, and I have authority to take it up again. This commandment I have received from My Father.”
Division arose again among the Jewish leaders because of these words.
Many among them said, “He has a demon and is insane. Why are you listening to him?”
Others said, “These words are not those of one possessed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
At that time, in Jerusalem, the Festival of Dedication of the Temple was being held. It was the winter season,
and Jesus was walking in the temple courts, in the porch of Solomon.
The Jewish leaders therefore surrounded Him and said to Him, “How long will you keep our hearts unsettled? If you are the Christ, tell us straightforwardly.”
Jesus answered them, “I have already told you, but you do not believe. The works I do in the name of My Father testify concerning Me.
But you do not believe, because you are not My sheep.
My sheep listen to My voice, and I know them, and they follow Me.
I give them eternal life, and they will not perish forever, and no one is able to snatch them from My hand.
My Father, who has given them to Me, is greater than all things, and no one is able to snatch them from the Father's hand.
I and the Father are one and the same.”
The Jewish leaders therefore picked up stones again to stone Him.
Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works that come from the Father. For which of those works are you stoning Me?”
The Jewish leaders answered Him, “We are not stoning You for a good work, but for blasphemy, because You, being merely a man, set Yourself up as God.”
Jesus answered them, “Is it not written in your Law, ‘I have said, you are gods’?
If the Law calls those to whom the word of God came ‘gods’ — and Scripture cannot be overturned —
then the one whom the Father has sanctified and sent into the world — will you say that he blasphemes God, because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?
If I am not doing the works of My Father, then do not believe Me.
But if I am doing those works, even if you do not believe Me, then believe in those works, so that you may know and understand that the Father is in Me, and I am in the Father.”
They tried to seize Him again, but He escaped from their hand.
Jesus then crossed the Jordan River back to the place where John used to baptize at first, and stayed there.
Many people came to Him and said, “John did not perform any sign, but everything that John said about this honored one is true.”
And many people came to believe in Him there.
